title: Is the undocumented migrant counted?
content: Contrary to the claims that there could be 20 million “illegal Bangladeshi migrants” staying in India, an analysis based on Census data suggests that there is no surge of undocumented migrants from Bangladesh, at least between 1991 and 2011

title: When debris came crushing: How rains and undisposed mud led to a disaster in Wayanad
content: A devastating mudslide in Wayanad, Kerala, was triggered by heavy rains and poor construction practices, resulting in multiple casualties.

title: Cashew dept. Secretary tenders revised unconditional apology before Kerala High Court
content: In his affidavit, Mr. Biju admitted that on July 2, he had issued the order without “the degree of care, deliberation and application of mind” that he should have exercised.

title: They are self-styled youth icons: Supreme Court slams influencers Samay Raina, Ranveer Allahbadia
content: The plea flagged jokes made by "India's Got Latent" host Samay Raina and other social media influencers Vipun Goyal, Balraj Paramjeet Singh Ghai, Sonali Thakkar and Nishant Jagdish Tanwar

title: Telangana CM to lay foundation stone for Amazon Web Services data centre in Bharat Future City on July 15
content: The first phase of construction is scheduled to be completed within 24 months. The project is part of Amazon’s proposed ₹60,000-crore investment in Telangana over the next decade

title: ECI extends SIR in Andhra Pradesh: enumeration till July 24, final roll on October 3
content: The draft publication of the electoral roll is now scheduled for July 31, 2026; the window for filing claims and objections will open from July 31 to August 30, 2026, with their disposal running from July 31 to September 28, 2026

title: Contractual appointment of ex-servicemen as gatemen at level crossings irks many
content: The Railway Board has approved the appointment of around 7,220 ex-servicemen on a contractual basis for the post of gateman in the Engineering department

title: MEA summons Iranian deputy envoy, after Indian national killed in attack on ship
content: MEA condemned the attacks on the commercial vessels MT Al Bahiyah and MT Mombasa, which had a total of 30 Indian seafarers onboard, and reiterated its call for an immediate cessation of violence and a return to dialogue and diplomacy to bring stability to West Asia

title: Taslima Nasrin to return to Kolkata after 19 years for anti-fundamentalism event
content: Taslima Nasrin shared a post on social media that she would be in Kolkata on August 1 to participate in an anti-fundamentalism literary event at Rabindra Sadan

title: Goondas Act invoked against Arasakumar
content: Arasakumar was recently arrested by the Central Crime Branch for allegedly cheating private schools across Tamil Nadu of about ₹100 crore by promising them government approval

title: Pakistan influence in West Asia setback to India, question mark on Vishwaguru's style: Congress
content: Congress general secretary Jairam Ramesh claimed that Islamabad's influence in West Asia is a "severe setback" to India

title: BJP urges CM Vijay to control TVK partymen, ensure safety of women in Tamil Nadu
content: Nainar Nagenthran referred to an incident in Karur district, in which a woman was stalked and harassed reportedly by a TVK functionary.

title: Retail inflation hits 18-month high of 4.4% in June 2026 on higher food and fuel prices
content: Looking ahead, economists predict that food inflation will increase in July as well due to a lacklustre monsoon

title: Why is Telangana still waiting for monsoon rain despite showers in neighbouring States?
content: International climate monitoring agencies continue to indicate that El Nino conditions are expected to strengthen during the remainder of the southwest monsoon season and later in the year, says IMD Hyderabad Senior Meteorologist Srinivas G.N.R.S

title: India bans the import of goods made using forced labour, even as U.S. investigation is pending
content: The U.S. is currently in the process of releasing its final decision on the tariffs it plans to levy on countries that it says have not done enough to stop the import of goods made using forced labour.

title: Why has India-U.S. trade deal been delayed? | Explained
content: From India’s Russian oil purchases to legal battles in Washington, the road to India-U.S. trade deal has become increasingly uncertain, and The Hindu explains why

title: Deal with U.S. ‘ready’ but comparative advantage aspect still being worked out, says Commerce Secretary
content: Regarding a timeline, Commerce Secretary Rajesh Agrawal indicated that the deal could be signed even before the U.S. investigations on its trade partners are completed.

title: Registration of car driven by Sub-Inspector’s six-year-old granddaughter cancelled for one year
content: The registrations of more than 4,050 vehicles found to have been driven by minors were cancelled across Telangana during the last financial year

title: Amid wrangle, LDF lays groundwork for CPI-CPI(M) detente over Kerala Deputy Opposition Leader post
content: T.P. Ramakrishnan, who held a brief closed-door meeting with the CPI State Secretary Binoy Viswam, signalled that the rapprochement was not immediately at hand. He said the discussions were in their nascent stages and denied any rift in the coalition. Viswam merely stated that “CPI and CPI(M) pursued the same politics”

title: Silk Saree that fits into a matchbox presented to Goddess Bharamaraba
content: The saree measures 5.5 metres in length and 48 inches in width and weighs approximately 200 grams; designed in the traditional “Ikkat” pattern, the saree can easily fit into a small matchbox when folded, the weaver explained

title: Trial run of traffic diversions for Miyapur-Allwyn flyover exposes bottleneck, police propose additional service lane to tackle congestion
content: Existing carriageway at Miyapur junction could not handle diverted traffic on a corridor used by nearly five lakh vehicles every day

title: Remains of three Andhra tourists killed in Vietnam boat accident brought home
content: Minister Kondapalli Srinivas stayed at the Shamshabad airport through Monday night, receiving the remains flown in via Mumbai and consoling the families before the bodies were sent to Kadapa, Machilipatnam and Hindupur

title: Pay ₹1 crore to each fisherman’s family, give new boats: Jagan Mohan Reddy in Visakhapatnam
content: Visiting the bereaved families in Visakhapatnam, the YSRCP president alleged the six deaths could have been averted, and said the ₹10 lakh announced was too little

title: Authorities demolish alleged illegal Eidgah built on graveyard land in U.P.'s Sambhal
content: The action was carried out by the revenue department with the help of a bulldozer following an order passed by the tehsildar’s court under provisions of the Uttar Pradesh Revenue Code for removal of the alleged encroachment

title: Immense potential for cooperation among BRICS countries in labour-related areas: Union Minister Mandaviya
content: The BRICS countries together represent nearly half of humanity and one of the world's largest labour forces says, Union Labour Minister Mandaviya

title: No rift in NCP, Sachchidanand Singh's letter carries no weight: Praful Patel
content: The letter written by Sachchidanand Singh carries no weight. Any issue should be resolved amicably in the interest of the party, says NCP national working president Praful Patel

title: India supports UN membership for Palestine, says MEA
content: India’s position on Palestine was apprised by Sripriya Ranganathan, a secretary at the External Affairs Ministry, during a ministerial meeting of the Palestine Donor Group, held in Brussels July 13

title: Dara Singh, prime convict in murder of Australian Christian missionary Graham Staines, two sons, to set free
content: Odisha State Sentence Review Board has recommended Dara Singh’s release on the grounds of ‘good behaviour’

title: India Kanoon challenges Delhi HC ruling recognising ‘right to be forgotten’, says it undermines open justice
content: The appeal challenged the single-judge judgment that allowed individuals to seek removal of personal information from search engine results, including legal databases, where such information is ‘no longer relevant or serves no legitimate public purpose’

title: Bhojshala complex case: Supreme Court suggests temporary prayer space for Muslims until case decided
content: The Bench further prohibited the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) from allowing any structural alterations to be made to the disputed structure without the prior permission of the apex court.

title: Delhi High Court to hear Indian Polo Association's plea over eviction from Jaipur Polo Ground on August 12
content: Justice Shankar said he was willing to hear the petition on Tuesday, but deferred the hearing on a request by the lawyers

title: SIT reconstructs minor rape-murder crime scene in Baruipur
content: A forensic team accompanied the investigators and collected fresh samples from the spot as part of the ongoing probe

title: KAU organises financial literacy programme for farmers in collaboration with RBI and Canara Bank
content: The programme was conducted as part of the Experiential Learning Programme of the final-year B.Sc. (Hons.) Co-operation & Banking students with the objective of disseminating financial literacy among farmers

title: 12 bikers, one SUV-owner fined for illegal off-roading in protected wildlife areas in Ladakh
content: As many as 12 bikers have been fined ₹10,000 each for illegally venturing into protected ecologically-sensitive zones near Lake Moriri, says official spokesman

title: Centre allots ₹1,000 crore for rural roads, sanctions building one lakh houses: Bengal CM
content: The Centre has given West Bengal ₹1,000 crore under the Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana in the first phase for construction of 2,400 km roads, says Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari

title: Wayanad debris slip: Why Kerala Minister said it was not a natural disaster
content: Kerala Agriculture Minister T. Siddique said the debris slip in Wayanad was caused by unscientifically stacked excavated soil, alleging that the contractor’s failure to remove the accumulated earth in time led to the incident

title: Direct tax mop-up grows 16% to over ₹6.51 lakh crore so far in FY27
content: Net corporate tax collection rose 22% to ₹2.40 lakh crore, and mop-up from net non-corporate tax (NCT), which includes taxes paid by individuals, HUFs, and firms, grew about 12 per cent to over ₹3.84 lakh crore till July 13

title: Gas pipeline leak during roadwork in Kerala capital sparks panic
content: The leak, which occurred on KIMS Road at Kumarapuram, was soon brought under control by Fire and Rescue Services personnel. Officials have confirmed that there is presently no danger to the public

title: Govt. Model Engineering College, Thrikkakara records more than 500 placements
content: Faculty members said the average annual pay package offered this time was around ₹8.3 lakh while the highest annual salary offered was ₹33 lakh

title: Aavin to reintroduce ‘button paal’ system of selling milk through vending machine, Madras High Court told
content: The pilot project is all set to be launched soon in Kodaikanal and Udhagamandalam following a Madras High Court directive

title: Bengaluru restaurants can remain open till 3.30 a.m. for FIFA football world cup matches
content: The order has been issued by Bengaluru City Police Commissioner Seemant Kumar Singh following a representation from the National Restaurant Association of India (NRAI)

title: Uproar in Chhattisgarh assembly over alleged shortage of fertilisers for farmers
content: Former Chief Minister raised the issue during Zero Hour, alleging that farmers were facing multiple challenges during the ongoing kharif paddy season

title: Police Sub-inspector accused of dowry harassment, assault; wife approaches Karnataka DG&IGP to lodge complaint
content: She alleged that despite approaching Kengeri Police Station, her complaint was not registered as an FIR, and was instead reduced to an NCR (Non-Cognizable Report)

title: 60 years after disappearing from Gir, Indian Grey Hornbills breed for fourth consecutive year
content: Study documents the successful establishment of the reintroduced population, with breeding recorded every year since the birds were released in 2021

title: Ajit Pawar: Baramati plane crash abruptly ends the decades-long political career of influential NCP leader
content: Ajit Pawar entered the Maharashtra Assembly in 1991, went on to become the Baramati MLA for multiple terms, and served as the State’s Deputy Chief Minister on several occasions

title: Why Tamil Nadu does not need privatisation of power distribution
content: Government funding narrows the gap between average cost of supply and average revenue realisation of the TNPDCL, says the White Paper

title: ED receives copies of documents in alleged ‘horse-trading’ case filed by TVK MLA
content: The agency is likely to probe the money laundering angle in the case. The police had seized ₹60 lakh in unaccounted cash from one of the suspects

title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay launches ‘Nalam TN’ portal
content: He also inaugurated health infrastructure projects and handed over appointment orders during a function at Presidency College, Chennai

title: IAS officers reshuffle in Tamil Nadu: Shilpa Prabhakar replaces Umanath as T.N. Civil Supplies Corporation MD
content: TNMSC MD H. Krishnanunni posted as Director of Animal Husbandry and Veterinary Services; S.P. Amrith replaces Mr. Krishnaunni; Labour Commissioner Shilpa Prabhakar Satish replaces P. Umanath as MD of Tamil Nadu Civil Supplies Corporation

title: Nagaland IED blast: NSCN vows to identify perpetrators of blast that killed Assam Rifles havildar
content: The armed group condemns the incident and says it will continue to honour the letter and spirit of the ceasefire ground rules

title: Tamil Nadu government transfers DVAC chief A. Arun; appoints IG Mageshwari
content: A. Arun was appointed as the Director of the DVAC on May 25 and has now been transferred within two months of taking charge

title: 'Systemic exploitation': India urges world to hold Pak 'fully accountable' over PoK unrest
content: India stated Pakistan's exploitation causes protests in Pakistan-occupied Jammu and Kashmir.  The ministry of external affairs spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal accused Islamabad of excessive force against public dissent.  India maintains Pakistan-occupied Jammu and Kashmir is an integral part of India.  New Delhi also accused Pakistan of human rights violations in the region.

title: 'Had engine problem': Vlogger Sourav Joshi backtracks on E20 petrol mileage claim
content: YouTuber Sourav Joshi clarified his Mercedes-Benz SUV mileage concerns after switching to E20 petrol. Mercedes-Benz India stated all its BS VI petrol vehicles are compatible with E20 fuel. Joshi later explained an engine issue caused the low mileage, not the fuel itself. He apologized for any misunderstanding and trimmed the controversial vlog segment. The company reiterated its commitment to customer safety and sustainable mobility.

title: From Rahul to Vijay to Nadda: CJP seeks support for NEET protesters
content: The Cockroach Janta Party has invited political leaders to join a protest at Jantar Mantar. This demonstration supports students protesting the alleged NEET UG examination irregularities and paper leak. Leaders from various parties have been urged to stand in solidarity with the protesting youth. The organization seeks accountability and justice for students affected by the education system. Many leaders have already offered support, while others are still being contacted.

title: Top computer scientist: ChatGPT, Claude and Gemini don't understand a word they say
content: Computer scientist Peter J. Denning argues Alan Turing's 1950 paper sent AI down the wrong road. In his new book, he says two Turing assumptions—that intelligence needs no body, and that imitation proves thought—have misled 75 years of research. Denning names ChatGPT, Claude and Gemini as systems that manipulate words without understanding them, and warns that agentic machines may prove more dangerous than superintelligence itself.

title: Jasprit Bumrah scripts history, surpasses Ravindra Jadeja to become ...
content: Bumrah reached the milestone in the opening ball of the 14th over of England's innings. He forced Brook into a tentative shot with a slower delivery that climbed on the batter, and Rohit Sharma completed a catch at first slip. The wicket was also Bumrah's 150th in ODI cricket.

title: Two things my DNA told me that eleven years of blood tests never could
content: Genetic sequencing revealed unique responses to rosuvastatin medication for Nickhil Jakatdar. His body processes the statin more slowly than average, influencing future treatment decisions. A variant associated with Gilbert syndrome was also identified through his genome. This finding indicated that prolonged intermittent fasting might not be universally beneficial for him. These nuanced genetic insights provided confidence to tailor his health approach.

title: Jeff Bezos’ wife Lauren Sánchez leads $100M investment to transform 8 US cities
content: Lauren Sanchez Bezos is directing significant Bezos Earth Fund investments. The fund is transforming urban spaces into green community hubs. This initiative aims to connect people and nature within cities. The Bezos Earth Fund also supports marine protection and AI climate solutions. Overall, the fund continues its commitment to climate and nature initiatives.

title: 'Learning a language never goes waste': SC declines to stay CBSE's 3-language policy
content: The Supreme Court declined to halt the CBSE's three-language policy for 2026-27. It will hear detailed challenges to the policy next week. Petitioners argued against the policy's implementation and lack of resources. The court noted that learning languages is beneficial and agreed to hear arguments. The Union government was directed to file its response within ten days.

title: 4,000-year-old noodles found in China challenge food history
content: Archaeologists discovered 4000-year-old noodles at the Lajia archaeological site. These ancient strands were found preserved inside an upside-down bowl under sediment. This find shifted the chronological axis of noodle history to a much earlier period. Researchers debated the composition and preparation technique of these ancient millet noodles. The discovery reinforced China's significant role in the early history of noodles.

title: Workers enter ‘forever layoff’ era as firms cut jobs while hiring for AI
content: Microsoft and other tech giants are implementing frequent workforce reductions.  These companies are simultaneously increasing investments in artificial intelligence technology.  Many firms are reorganizing operations to adapt to the evolving technological landscape.  This trend reflects a broader industry shift towards continuous workforce adjustments.  Employees face uncertainty as businesses navigate AI integration and productivity demands.

title: 'London Rule' that explains why AI researchers are leaving Google for Anthropic & OpenAI
content: Four senior AI researchers have left Google DeepMind in under two weeks, joining rivals Anthropic and OpenAI. The common thread isn't pay—it's London. UK non-compete rules, enforceable unlike in California, explain why Nobel laureate John Jumper won't start at Anthropic until next year. With compute shifting to Mountain View and pre-IPO equity on offer, Google's London research bench is quietly draining westward.

title: Jammu court summons crime branch officer in Rs 500-crore Vaishno Devi row
content: A Jammu court has ordered an inquiry officer to appear with records concerning alleged fake silver offerings. Approximately 20 tonnes of silver offerings were reportedly found to be mostly inferior metals. An advocate filed a complaint seeking an FIR and investigation into the alleged misappropriation. The court directed the inquiry officer to present relevant records on July 29, 2026.

title: 'Vessels carried 30 Indian seafarers': MEA condemns Hormuz attack that killed sailor
content: India registered a strong protest against continued attacks on vessels carrying Indian seafarers. Two vessels, MT Al Bahiyah and MT Mombasa, were attacked in the Strait of Hormuz. One Indian national tragically lost his life while others sustained injuries during the attacks. The ministry summoned Iran's deputy chief of mission to lodge a strong protest. India calls for an immediate cessation of violence and a return to dialogue.

title: Weather tomorrow: IMD warns of extremely heavy rain in 14 states
content: Northeast and East India will experience extremely heavy rainfall over the next week. Southern and coastal states are expected to face continued hot and humid weather conditions. Uttarakhand and parts of North India will also receive heavy rainfall starting mid-July. Telangana authorities are preparing for potential El Niño impacts on agriculture. The monsoon remains active over eastern regions, bringing significant precipitation.

title: NHL Trade Rumors: Maple Leafs urged to acquire $92.8M forward for Cup run
content: Trade speculation surrounding Elias Pettersson continues to grow as the Toronto Maple Leafs search for another top-six center. Although his eight-year, $92.8 million contract presents a major cap challenge, reports suggest the Vancouver Canucks are open to moving him for a relatively modest return. Analysts believe Toronto could benefit from placing Pettersson behind Auston Matthews while shifting John Tavares into a more suitable role. Whether the financial pieces can be aligned remains the biggest question, but this is one of the most intriguing NHL trade rumors to watch this offseason.

title: Expired Maggi sold by Vishal Mega Mart leaves man ill; court imposes Rs 2.8 lakh penalty
content: A consumer commission found Vishal Mega Mart liable for selling expired noodles. The customer suffered food poisoning after consuming the product purchased in March 2025. The store failed to appear before the commission to contest the allegations. Vishal Mega Mart must pay compensation and punitive damages to the affected consumer. The commission also ordered a deposit into the Chief Minister's Relief Fund.

title: Jos Buttler enters elite club, becomes only the second England player to...
content: Jos Buttler achieved his 200th One-Day International cap for England. He reached this significant milestone during the opening ODI against India. Former captain Eoin Morgan holds the record for most ODIs played. England batted first after winning the toss at Edgbaston. India welcomed back senior players like Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma.

title: 'Sensitive matter': SC orders separate namaz space near Bhojshala; flags MP govt's lax law & order
content: The Supreme Court directed that any ASI structural alterations require its prior permission in the Bhojshala row. Separate space for Friday prayers for Muslims adjacent to the site was also directed. Both Hindu and Muslim sides were urged to exercise restraint during proceedings.

title: 'Dil mein Babar, munh mein Ram': Anti-SP posters ignite fresh political storm in UP
content: Posters targeting Mulayam Singh Yadav and Akhilesh Yadav appeared across Uttar Pradesh. The Samajwadi Party accused the BJP of inciting communal sentiments with these displays. Uttar Pradesh minister Om Prakash Rajbhar defended the posters as appropriate responses. Samajwadi Party workers removed the controversial hoardings in several locations. Police are examining CCTV footage to identify those responsible for the posters.

title: India tour of Bangladesh gets closer to approval; Afghanistan T20Is could be pushed back
content: India's white-ball tour of Bangladesh is edging closer to approval, with the BCCI exploring a revised international schedule. To accommodate the six-match series, India's proposed T20Is against Afghanistan are likely to be pushed back by a few days, while the BCB remains optimistic about hosting the Men in Blue after a four-year gap.

title: 'Dhamaal 4' BO Day 5 [LIVE]: Inches towards Rs 80 crore on first Tuesday
content: 'Dhamaal 4', led by Ajay Devgn, Arshad Warsi, Riteish Deshmukh and Jaaved Jaaferi, is closing out its opening week at the box office. After a stellar Day 3 that brought in Rs 28.50 crore, the film saw a sharp 69% drop on Day 4, and is now tracking modest numbers on Day 5 as it heads toward the Rs 75 crore domestic net mark.

title: UK seeks law change to deport Pakistani rapist and grooming gang leader
content: The UK government is changing a law that prevents serious criminals' deportation.  Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood announced Parliament will amend the Immigration Act 1971.  This change targets individuals like grooming gang leader Shabir Ahmed.  Ahmed was jailed for rape and sexual offenses against young girls.  The government seeks to remove legal protections for such serious offenders.

title: Trump tariffs struck down by US Supreme Court: How much has been refunded so far
content: The Supreme Court's recent decision led the US government to refund a staggering eighty-one billion dollars in tariffs, overturning many of President Donald Trump's imposed tariffs. This fiscal year has already seen a notable surge in refunds according to Treasury budget data. A ruling from the US Court of International Trade in May also challenged these blanket tariffs.

title: Kargil War: How July 14 turned the tide and put Pakistan on back foot
content: The Indian Army marked July 14, 1999, as a decisive turning point in the Kargil War. This date saw India regain control of strategic heights and Pakistan announce troop withdrawal. The Fire and Fury Corps highlighted exceptional courage and coordinated operations during this period. Prime Minister Atal Bihari Vajpayee declared Operation Vijay a success on this significant day.

title: Shah Rukh Khan gets relief as SC clears way for Mannat's two additional floors
content: The Supreme Court has dismissed a petition challenging the Coastal Regulation Zone clearance. This ruling allows Shah Rukh Khan to proceed with renovations at his Mumbai home. The apex court declined to interfere with an earlier National Green Tribunal order. Activist Santosh Daundkar's appeal was rejected by the bench. The legal dispute over Mannat's expansion has now concluded.

title: Drunk cop goes on driving rampage, injures 2 on Delhi-Lucknow Highway
content: A suspended police constable drove drunk, injuring two pedestrians on the Delhi-Lucknow Highway. He continued speeding after hitting them, even with a detached tire. Motorists and residents chased and stopped the vehicle, then assaulted the constable. Police rescued him and took him into custody for further investigation. The injured victims are receiving medical treatment at a local hospital.

title: Builder took Rs 20 lakh but never built the apartment; homebuyers win refund with interest
content: A Kolkata developer must refund over twenty lakh rupees to a couple. The couple cancelled their flat booking after construction stalled for years. The consumer commission found the developer liable for deficiency in service. The developer failed to provide any explanation for the refund delay. The commission ordered the refund with interest and litigation costs.

title: 'Super El Niño' brewing in Pacific: Scientists eye cloud hacking
content: An Super El Nino event is emerging over the Pacific Ocean, with forecasts suggesting a period of extreme weather conditions and fluctuations in food prices worldwide. Scientists are investigating geoengineering as a potential remedy to lessen its adverse effects. Meanwhile, immediate action involves enhancing readiness and cutting emissions to address the anticipated consequences.

title: 20km above ground, always watching: How India’s new airships will snoop across the border
content: India is developing indigenous stratospheric airships for persistent border surveillance. These platforms will provide round-the-clock intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance capabilities. They will operate at high altitudes, extending communications and electronic snooping abilities. This initiative supports the armed forces' modernization drive for enhanced target acquisition. The program aims to create a fail-proof and redundant network of surveillance assets.

title: Virat Kohli, Gautam Gambhir avoid interaction ahead of India vs England 1st ODI
content: Head coach Gautam Gambhir and Virat Kohli were not seen interacting during India's training. Gambhir spent considerable time with other squad members and coaching staff. Kohli engaged in a lengthy batting session and spoke with batting coach Sitanshu Kotak. Captain Shubman Gill and Rohit Sharma also had discussions with the head coach.

title: Navi Mumbai: Man killed, body chopped into pieces; wife, lover held 11 months after murder
content: Police arrested a wife and her paramour for allegedly killing her husband.  The accused murdered the man last August and dismembered his body.  They then dumped the remains in a forest in Navi Mumbai.  The crime came to light after the victim's brother grew suspicious.  Remains were recovered, and the accused are in police custody.

title: Ram Charan, Upasana buy luxury farmhouse; plans organic farming
content: Actor Ram Charan and his wife Upasana have acquired a new luxurious farmhouse. This property is situated on five acres of land outside Hyderabad city. The couple is planning a housewarming ceremony for their new private retreat. Ram Charan also intends to use some land for organic farming initiatives. The farmhouse features premium interiors designed by Aamir and Hameeda Sharma.

title: Anupam Kher defends Ram Mandir remarks amid backlash: 'I'm not scared'
content: Actor Anupam Kher has refused to retract his controversial remarks on alleged Ram Mandir donation irregularities. He stated his words were taken out of context and insists he spoke with honesty. Kher asserted that external pressure will not alter his firm stance on the matter. He clarified that his comments were made responsibly and without fear of backlash. The actor remains committed to speaking his truth regardless of public agreement.

title: 'Taunted woman, harassed': Nashik family chased for 15km after altercation with group
content: A family visiting Bhavli Dam faced harassment from local men. An argument escalated after a woman was taunted by some individuals. More locals joined the group and confronted the visiting family members. The family was then chased for many kilometers by the group. Police have since arrested some individuals in connection with this incident.

title: 'Meri poori duniya chali gayi': Ketan Agarwal’s mother writes to PM Modi, seeks justice
content: Ketan Agarwal's mother has written an emotional letter to Prime Minister Narendra Modi. She is seeking justice and the strictest action against those responsible for his brutal murder. The family has suffered immense pain and an irreparable void since Ketan's untimely death. Investigators are examining complicated personal circumstances and alleged double lives in the ongoing probe. The grieving family continues to await justice for their beloved son Ketan.

title: 'Not a brick in my name': Ram Temple treasurer amid donation theft row
content: Ayodhya Ram Temple Trust treasurer Swami Govind Dev Giri denied resignation reports. He stated that the theft of donations is likely around Rs 3 crore. Champat Rai resigned voluntarily after realizing his negligence in the matter. An Ayodhya court extended the judicial custody of eight accused individuals. The Supreme Court has also issued notices regarding the alleged misappropriation of funds.

title: Lights out: How Iran quietly moved millions of barrels through Hormuz under America's nose
content: According to ship-tracking data, six US-sanctioned supertankers with a carrying capacity of 12 million barrels of crude combined have crossed the Strait of Hormuz into the Gulf of Oman over the past week while keeping their transponders switched off.

title: 6 Civilians Killed After Pakistan Forces Open Fire During Clashes In PoK
content: Among those killed in Baloch Sadhunati district were Zahid Mughal, Zafar Mughal and Arsalan Akbar. The latest violence has further intensified tensions in the region, where resentment against Islamabad has been steadily increasing.

title: Opinion: The Politics Of Satluj: A Film That No Party Can Fully Own
content: Satluj has already achieved one political outcome: it has forced every major party to revisit questions that have remained politically unresolved for four decades

title: US-Iran War News LIVE: Global Crude Oil Prices Reach Highest In A Month As Middle East Conflict Reignites
content: US-Iran War Live Updates: The flare-up is the latest to undermine an interim agreement between Washington and Tehran aimed at ending their war, which has caused global economic shockwaves since it began in late February.

title: PM Modi To Launch Rs 2,500-Crore Projects In Punjab On Friday
content: These healthcare projects worth nearly Rs 1,200 crore will help to further advanced medical treatment, research and education in northern India.

title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Sensex Closes 560 Points Lower As Oil Prices Rally Amid US-Iran Tensions
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty Highlights: Crude oil prices jump to one-month high after 10% rally; Brent oil near $85 a barrel.

title: Delhi's New Policy Paves Way For High-Rises. Architects Flag One Big Risk
content: Without parallel investments in roads and urban design, higher density could simply place more pressure on already strained neighbourhoods.

title: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella's Sharp Warning for Companies Using AI
content: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella in a personal blog post pointed out that companies were actually paying for AI usage "twice".

title: Trump Wants To Pass Crypto Bill As Tribute To Lindsey Graham: What Is It
content: The Clarity Act is proposed US federal legislation designed to create a comprehensive regulatory framework for cryptocurrencies and digital assets.

title: Telangana Engineer Caught Accepting Rs 2 Lakh, Rs 32 Lakh Seized From Home
content: The accused, identified as 33-year-old Kallepalli Sathish Kumar, was working at the Municipal Office in Telangana's Peddapally district

title: Assam Town Puts Public Urinators On Blast With 'Hall of Shame' Campaign
content: The municipality's "Hall of Shame" campaign uses CCTV cameras installed at several locations across Tinsukia to identify people allegedly caught urinating in public or littering

title: US To Witness Sweltering Heat, Record Overnight Temperatures This Week
content: A relentless heat dome will keep overnight temperatures dangerously high across the US this week, raising heatstroke risk for millions nationwide

title: In A First For Kerala, Jailed BJP Councillor Takes Oath Inside Prison
content: The oath was administered following a Kerala High Court directive, with R Sugathan remaining in judicial custody under the Kerala Anti-Social Activities (Prevention) Act, or KAAPA.

title: India's Strong Message To Iran After Sailor's Death In Hormuz Ship Strike
content: According to the report, the tankers, Mombasa and Al Bahiyah, were struck while transiting the southern passage of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Beyond Low Cost: Why Foreigners Choose India's Healthcare Over The West
content: India's medical tourism industry is estimated to be worth $12.32 billion in 2026. It is growing much faster than the country's overall tourism sector.

title: From A "Hello... This is Divya" WhatsApp Text To A Rs 21-Crore Crypto Fraud
